# pfsense_firewall_nat_one_to_one_rules (Data Source)

Retrieves all [NAT 1:1](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/nat/1-1.html) rules.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "pfsense_firewall_nat_one_to_one_rules" "all" {}

output "rule_count" {
  value = length(data.pfsense_firewall_nat_one_to_one_rules.all.rules)
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `rules` (List of Object) List of NAT 1:1 rules. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules))

<a id="nestedatt--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Read-Only:

- `description` (String) Description used as the unique identifier for this NAT 1:1 rule.
- `destination_address` (String) Destination network. Can be 'any', a CIDR network, an alias, or a special pfSense interface address. Defaults to 'any'.
- `destination_not` (Boolean) Invert the destination address match.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable this 1:1 NAT rule.
- `external` (String) External subnet IP for the 1:1 mapping.
- `interface` (String) Network interface this 1:1 NAT rule applies to (e.g. 'wan', 'lan', 'opt1').
- `ipprotocol` (String) IP protocol. Options: 'inet', 'inet6', 'inet46'.
- `nat_reflection` (String) NAT reflection mode.
- `no_binat` (Boolean) Do not create a binat entry for this rule (one-way 1:1 NAT).
- `source_address` (String) Source network.
- `source_not` (Boolean) Invert the source address match.
